metadata.title: Agenda Item F – Funding Awards for Drinking Water State Revolving Fund Lead Service Line Replacement

sections.title:
State Water Infrastructure Authority
Meeting Date: December 10, 2025

elements.text:
Agenda Item F – Funding Awards for Drinking Water State Revolving Fund Lead Service Line 
Replacement
Division of Water Infrastructure Staff Report

content.text:
funding scenario proposed for consideration by the North 
Carolina State Water Infrastructure Authority (Authority). Subsequent updates and corrections 
to the application information and funding scenario (including application scores, ranking, 
potential funding amount, etc.) may occur and will be presented to the Authority during the 
December 10 meeting. This staff report represents applications received as of 
November 3, 2025, for Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) Drinking Water State 
Revolving Fund Lead Service Line Replacement (DWSRF-LSLR) funding. Applications are selected 
for funding by the Authority during the meeting, and the Authority’s selections are final.

Congress appropriated funds to the Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF) in the IIJA of 
2021. The IIJA appropriated funds for five fiscal years (FY 2022 – FY 2026) specifically for the 
purpose of inventorying and replacing lead service lines (LSLs). These funds are referred to as 
the DWSRF-LSLR funds.

North Carolina’s FY 2024 DWSRF-LSLR allotment is $76,201,000. No state match is required for 
this funding. A portion of the allotment, up to 26 percent ($19,812,260) will be used for set-
asides.

The draft DWSRF-LSLR program’s Intended Use Plan (IUP) for FY 2024 funds was published for 
public comment on February 28, 2025, and public comments were due by March 14, 2025. At 
its April 2024 meeting, the Authority approved a final Priority Rating System (PRS) for DWSRF-
LSLR funds after the public comment period.

Funding awards will be approved by the Authority at its December 10, 2025, meeting and are 
contingent upon funds being released by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (USEPA).

Table 1 on the next page shows the FY 2024 DWSRF-LSLR funding awarded at the Authority 
meetings in the year 2025 and funding that remains available for award. Note that more funds 
may be awarded at any Authority meeting than what is presented in the table.
IIJA DWSRF-LSLR Funds Awarded and Available by Funding Round

elements.caption: Table 1. FY 2024¹ IIJA DWSRF-LSLR Funds Awarded and Available by Funding Round
elements.summary: Table showing SWIA Meeting Award Date, Principal Forgiveness (PF) Available or Awarded, No-interest Loan Available or Awarded, and Total Available or Awarded (includes PF).

content.text: Additional funds to be awarded may be added to FY 2024 funds due to declinations of previous awards.

To be determined based on remaining availability of FY 2024 funds after September 2025 awards. It will be 
supplemented with additional funding from FY 2025 funds if demand exceeds availability of FY 2024 funds.

The following maximum DWSRF-LSLR funding loan amounts, including principal forgiveness
(PF), apply per applicant for each round of funding awarded at Authority meetings:
Replacement projects: $5 million for projects replacing known LSLs.

Find-and-Replace projects: $2 million for projects finding and replacing LSLs in areas 
suspected of having LSLs. These projects must result in service lines of unknown 
material within the project area being categorized as “known lead” or “known not lead” 
using methods approved for completion of the LSL inventory as required in the Lead and 
Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR). The budget must limit service line material verification 
(i.e., non-replacement activities) to $1 million.
Inventory-only projects: $1 million for projects to conduct an LSL inventory.

The maximum loan amount per applicant per round can exceed the funding cap if additional 
funding is available in the funding round as indicated in Table 1. Maximum loan amounts will be 
increased by $500,000 increments, up to the funding amount requested by the applicant, in 
priority order until all funds available during the funding round are awarded.

The following steps are used to determine PF awards in the proposed preliminary funding 
scenario:

Per the DWSRF-LSLR IUP, starting with the highest-scoring application, PF and repayable 
loan funds are applied to all projects eligible for PF to either fully fund the projects or up 
to the applicable funding cap.

In accordance with the IUP, if additional PF funds remains available after all projects are
awarded PF up to the respective percent PF eligibility, then additional PF can be 
awarded above the PF eligibility in ten-percent increments, up to 100 percent of the 
funding requested for applicants that qualified for PF. Due to the availability of PF funds 
for this round, all projects eligible for PF would be provided with additional ten-percent 
PF increments, up to 100 percent.

Table 2 shows the number of new applications received, number of applications reconsidered 
because they were either not funded or only partially funded in the previous round, and the 
funding requested as of November 3, 2025, divided by project type.
Table 2. Summary of Project Applications Considered

elements.caption: Table 2. Summary of Project Applications Considered
elements.summary: Table summarizing Project Applications Considered, showing counts for Replace Known LSLs, Find and Replace, Inventory, and Total for Applications Received and Applications Reconsidered from previous Round, along with Funding Requested.

subsections.title: Proposed Funding Scenario for the DWSRF-LSLR Program

content.text:
In the preliminary funding scenario presented, ten of the 11 new funding applications received 
by November 3, 2025, are proposed for funding. No applications had to be reconsidered from 
the previous funding round.
Town of Dallas Lead and Copper Service Replacement

, is not included 
in the proposed funding scenario because it is a duplicate of a previous application that was 
awarded funding in full at the September 17, 2025, meeting of the Authority. No additional 
funds were needed by the Applicant.

All projects included in the proposed funding scenario would be fully funded. No project would 
be partially funded.

Six project applications qualify for PF. Due to the availability of PF funds, additional PF could be
awarded to these applications following the procedures listed above, and all six projects are 
included in the proposed funding scenario to receive 100-percent PF funding awards.

In the proposed funding scenario, a total of $9,119,300 would be awarded, $5,507,500 of which 
would be in PF and $3,611,800 in zero-percent interest loans. Table 3 below summarizes the 
proposed preliminary funding scenario.
Table 3. Proposed Funding Scenario for December 10, 2025, Awards

elements.caption: Table 3. Proposed Funding Scenario for December 10, 2025, Awards
elements.summary: Table summarizing the Proposed Funding Scenario for December 10, 2025, Awards, showing Funding Application Projects, Eligible Applications Considered, Funded Applications in Scenario, Proposed Principal Forgiveness, Proposed Zero-interest Loan, and Proposed Total Funding.

elements.text: Project-Specific Notes for Applications in the Proposed Funding Scenario

content.text:
The Inventory Reserve amount of $7,577,793 (i.e., 50 percent of the total available funds for 
this round) was not met for this round. However, since all applications for inventory-type
projects for this round would be fully funded in the proposed funding scenario, the 
remaining funds from the Inventory Reserve were made available to other project types.

Two funding applications were received from local government units designated as 
Distressed, the Town of Pink Hill and the Town of Micro. Both applications are included in 
the proposed funding scenario to be fully funded with a 100-percent PF award.

Table 4 on the next page summarizes the funds available for this round, the proposed funding 
scenario recommended for awards at the December 10, 2025, meeting, and how the 
recommendation will impact future funding awards.
Table 4. FY2024 IIJA DWSRF-LSLR Funds Available for Future Funding Rounds

content.text:
Additional funds from the IIJA DWSRF-LSLR FY2025 allotment may be awarded to meet demand once all of the 
FY2024 funds have been awarded

Table 5 on the next page identifies the projects that would be awarded funding under this
proposed preliminary funding scenario.

A full list of applications is shown in Attachment F-1. The Application Number in the first 
column in the table below refers to the Application Number in the attached DWSRF-LSLR 
spreadsheet.
Staff can assist the Authority with other scenarios during the meeting.

Note that all awards from the DWSRF-LSLR Funds are contingent on the Division’s receipt of the 
LSLR funds from the US EPA.
